headline: Australia Consumer Confidence Posts Biggest Fall Since Covid
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Australian consumer sentiment tumbled the most since 2020 while business confidence also nosedived as soaring fuel prices coupled with higher interest rates triggered dramatic falls in both readings.Consumer sentiment plummeted 12.5% to 80.1 points in April, Westpac Banking Corp. said in a statement on Tuesday. Near-term expectations dropped back to 2022–23 lows that were last seen during the post-Covid “cost of living” crisis.“A sharp deterioration in expectations suggests consum

headline: How The Cactus (WHD) Narrative Is Shifting With Expansion Plans And Post Earnings Volatility
summary: Cactus’s latest analyst update keeps the Fair Value price target steady at US$56.56, signaling no change to the central valuation anchor in the current model. That stability comes even as bullish and cautious voices debate the impact of international expansion, the Surface Pressure Control acquisition, and recent post earnings trading on where the stock could go next. Read on to see what is driving these views and how you can track the evolving narrative around Cactus from here. Analyst Price...

headline: How The Tate & Lyle (LSE:TATE) Investment Story Is Shifting After Fresh Analyst Revisions
summary: Tate & Lyle is back in focus after analysts trimmed fair value estimates from £5.01 to £4.84 per share and, in some cases, reset formal price targets such as the move from £5.40 to £5.00. Those shifts are being framed less as a reaction to a single event and more as a recalibration of how much investors might reasonably pay, with bullish and bearish voices drawing different conclusions from the same set of adjustments. Read on to see what is driving this evolving narrative and how you can...

headline: Citigroup Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
summary: NEW YORK, April 14, 2026--Today Citigroup reported first quarter 2026 results, which can be found on its website at https://www.citigroup.com/global/investors. headline: Market Chaos Is A Boon for Wall Street Banks

headline: Citigroup Reports $22 Billion of Private-Credit Exposure
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Citigroup Inc. said its exposure to private-credit firms was $22 billion in the fourth quarter, offering a look at what has become a closely watched development by investors.The loans are part of the bank’s portfolio of lending to financial firms other than banks, which totaled $118 billion at the end of last year, Citigroup said Tuesday in a presentation.
